name: Long Point Tip
notes: Rocky tip creates a current seam that holds fish year-round. Early morning shallow bite (8-14ft) on the rocky edges is excellent May-September. Push deeper mid-day. Crayfish and goby patterns both work.

name: Long Point Inner Bay (Pottahawk Pt)
notes: Prime spawn flat — peaks early-mid June. Sheltered from NW wind. Early morning shallow bite is exceptional May-August in 4-10ft over rock/gravel. Drift live bait between Bait Island and Pottahawk. Crayfish patterns dominate here.

name: Bluff Bar (Long Point Offshore)
notes: Post-spawn holding area for bass leaving Long Point Inner Bay. Deep-summer structure. Top of the reef comes into 16-20ft — fish the edges early morning. Drop-shot goby or tube jigs. Key July-October.

name: Port Ryerse / Normandale Offshore
notes: First offshore staging area after post-spawn dispersal from Long Point Inner Bay. Rock humps in 20-35ft. Fish the top of humps in 15-20ft early morning. Drop-shot goby plastics or tube jigs July-October.

name: Turkey Point Reef
notes: Resident bass with reliable rocky structure. Protected from west wind — good in SW blow. Shallow rocky edges at 6-12ft fire early morning all summer. Multiple humps at 12-22ft for mid-day.

name: Wooley's Point / Nanticoke Offshore
notes: Prime mid-summer through fall. Former Nanticoke thermal discharge historically concentrates bait in shoulder seasons. Work the tops of humps in 15-22ft early morning. October fish stack here before winter. Best Aug-Oct.

name: Port Dover Offshore Reefs
notes: Rocky/rubble bottom 1-3 miles offshore in 12-30ft. Early morning on the shallower humps (12-18ft) is productive all summer. Convenient Port Dover launch. Tube jigs and drop-shot year round.

name: Grand River Mouth (Port Maitland)
notes: Grand River plume warms early in spring — top pre-spawn spot. Rocky substrate near mouth holds fish all season. Early morning shallow bite in 5-12ft is consistent summer through fall. Current creates feeding lanes — crayfish patterns work well near the mouth.

name: Mohawk Island Shoals
notes: Island creates multiple current breaks and wind-shadow zones. Rocky perimeter in 6-22ft. Shallow early morning bite (6-12ft) is reliable May-September on the current-washed edges. One of the most consistent multi-season spots on the Ontario shore.

name: Port Colborne / Welland Canal Mouth
notes: Canal mouth current concentrates baitfish year-round creating a consistent feeding lane. Early morning 6-12ft bite is very productive May-October. Gravelly Bay regulated zone east of breakwall — check Ontario regs. Diving crankbaits excel in current.

name: Sherkston Shoals
notes: Clean rocky shoal between Port Colborne and Crystal Beach. Early morning 6-12ft bite active all summer. Eastern basin clarity means fish spook easier in sun — first light window is critical here. Anchor or drift diving crankbaits over the flats.

name: Crystal Beach Offshore Break
notes: World-class smallmouth. The 18-22ft break is the mid-day money zone, but the 10-16ft rocky edge fires hard early morning all summer. August and October are the twin peaks. Crystal clear water — drop-shot with finesse goby plastics, lighter fluorocarbon. Best bite is first 2 hours of daylight.

name: Waverly Shoal (Fort Erie)
notes: Series of current-washed rocky humps near the Niagara outflow. Current keeps fish active and shallower than other eastern basin spots — 8-14ft early morning bite is reliable all summer. Reef tops come into 8-12ft. Near-Niagara current influence is a big advantage. Best Aug-Oct.

name: Upper Niagara River (Canadian Side)
notes: Current-driven river environment keeps fish active and shallower than lake spots — 8-14ft is productive morning to evening all summer. Current scours the rocky bottom creating goby habitat. June through November is exceptional. Check Canadian/Ontario regulations.

name: Upper Niagara River American Side (Strawberry Island Shallows)
notes: Strawberry Island–Motor Island Shallows: 400-acre littoral zone, Niagara River's largest. Rocky and gravelly bottom in 3-10ft — prime spawn flat May-June when water temps hit 62-65°F. Submergent aquatic vegetation (wild celery beds) hold crayfish. Current seams along island edges concentrate fish. Access from Beaver Island SP boat ramp (42.960°N, 78.950°W) or Isle View Park. Spinnerbaits and jerkbaits in the shallows. River current keeps fish active all summer. American Channel maintains 12ft minimum depth; stay in the 3-10ft edges for bass.
source: Wikipedia (Strawberry Island NY), NYSDEC, Bassmaster (Niagara smallies article), NY Coastal Habitat Rating Form

name: Black Rock Canal / Squaw Island Current Seam
notes: Black Rock Canal (3.5 miles, 21ft deep) connects Buffalo Harbor to Black Rock Lock where the Niagara River exits Lake Erie. May through early June is peak — bass stage here pre-spawn before moving up into the river shallows. Lock turbulence and current seam along Squaw Island's south tip creates a natural ambush zone. Current-washed rock riprap along canal walls holds fish. Protected from wind — fishable in most weather. Live shiners permitted from first Saturday in May. Also holds white bass, walleye, northern pike. Erie County fishing site #39 (Bird Island Pier) provides shore access at the southern tip of Unity Island.
source: NYSDEC Lake Erie Smallmouth Bass guide, Erie County Shore Fishing Sites, Wikipedia (Black Rock Lock), latitude.to

name: Buffalo Outer Harbor / North Breakwall
notes: 2,204-ft north breakwall at the mouth of Buffalo River where Lake Erie transitions to the Niagara River outflow. Riprap-covered breakwall face in 6-22ft holds gobies, crayfish, and perch — classic ambush structure for smallmouth. Best in spring (April-June) as fish stage before moving up into river, and again in fall (Sept-Oct) when cooling water pulls bass back shallow. Also fishes well for white bass and walleye. Buffalo Small Boat Harbor inside the walls provides a sheltered launch. The breakwall outer face is exposed to SW fetch — best fished in E or SE wind. Also referenced as 'better catches along deep edges of the Buffalo Harbor outer breakwalls' in NYSDEC reports.
source: US Lighthouses (Buffalo North Breakwater South Side Light: 42.87765N, 78.89007W), NYSDEC, Buffalo Blueway, Erie County fishing guide

name: Smokes Creek Mouth (Lackawanna)
notes: Smokes Creek discharges into Lake Erie at Lackawanna — the creek mouth plume warms early in spring and concentrates baitfish, drawing pre-spawn smallmouth. Rock, gravel, and sand substrate at the mouth is textbook spawning habitat. Walleye also active here (reported in 26-32ft range offshore of the mouth). Current fishing reports noted '4 lb.+ smallmouth' in the Smokes Creek area. NYSDEC fishing site nearby. Shore access limited but the mouth area is reachable by small boat from Buffalo Harbor (~2 miles south). Coordinates approximate creek mouth: 42.813°N, 78.833°W per USGS topo.
source: USGS topo (Smoke Creek topo map), NYSDEC Lake Erie reports, 1stclass-bass.com, Erie County shore fishing sites

name: Seneca Shoal (Buffalo Offshore)
notes: NYSDEC top-listed smallmouth hotspot offshore of Woodlawn/Buffalo area. Steep breaklines dropping from 12-19ft on the shoal top down to 32-35ft. Late May through late June: concentrate on rocky structure in 15-20ft. Mid-summer bite moves to 25-40ft on the deeper edges. Forum reports: 'fantastic bite in 25-45ft near structure such as reefs, rock piles and drop-offs' and 'good bite on crayfish at Seneca Shoal in 19-32ft.' 'Seneca Shoal has steep breaklines regarded as structure fishing habitat.' Seneca and Waverly shoals are among the 'giant reefs' in the NY eastern basin that 'come out of 35-40ft of water up to 12-15ft.' Coordinates from USGS GNIS / anyplaceamerica.com topo record.
source: NYSDEC Lake Erie Smallmouth Bass Hotspots, anyplaceamerica.com GNIS topo (42.7858919N, 78.8803124W), Lake Ontario United forum, ODU Magazine

name: Woodlawn Bar (Hamburg Offshore Shoal)
notes: NYSDEC-listed top smallmouth hotspot offshore of Woodlawn Beach State Park (park at 42.7902°N). One of the named rocky shoal systems in the NY eastern Lake Erie 'Buffalo to Angola' smallmouth hotspot map. Late May-late June: fish rocky structure in 15-20ft. Summer: push deeper to 20-30ft. Woodlawn Beach State Park (107 acres) on Lakeshore Road in Blasdell offers nearest shore reference. No public boat launch at Woodlawn — launch from Buffalo Small Boat Harbor (~3 mi north) or Sturgeon Point Marina (~7 mi south). Coordinates are estimated from geographic position between Seneca Shoal and Hamburg; precise chart position from NYSDEC hotspot map PDF (not georeferenced online). Treat as approximate center — adjust with Navionics or Garmin depth map on approach.
source: NYSDEC Lake Erie Smallmouth Bass Hotspots PDF (lakeeriesmb.pdf), Wikipedia Woodlawn Beach State Park (42.7902°N), ODU Magazine, NYSDEC seasonal reports

name: Myers Reef (Lake Erie Offshore)
notes: NYSDEC top-ranked summer smallmouth destination — 'traditionally among the top destinations for summer bass anglers.' Located ~5 miles offshore in the NY eastern basin. Structure rises from 35-40ft basin up to 12-15ft on the reef top — extreme relief creates strong thermocline and feeding concentration. Bass 'transition from shallow to deep end of the reef as summer progresses': early summer fish the 12-18ft tops, mid-late summer push to 25-40ft deep edges. Walleye also stack here, especially in shallows in late spring and along deep side in summer. Coordinates extracted from fishingstatus.com map embed (42.73833N, 78.95333W) — treat as approximate reef center. Use Navionics or Garmin depth chip to find the exact shoal top on approach.
source: NYSDEC Lake Erie Smallmouth Bass Hotspots, fishingstatus.com spot listing (IndexID 1420563), ODU Magazine, Lake Ontario United forum (32-36ft depth reports)

name: Evans Bar (Sturgeon Point Offshore)
notes: NYSDEC-listed top smallmouth hotspot. Shallow rocky nearshore reef located ~3 miles southwest of Sturgeon Point Harbor (Sturgeon Point at 42.6909°N, 79.0475°W). Structure described as 'shallow, rocky near-shore reef' and 'top smallmouth bass holding structure offering great bass action all season.' Nighttime walleye around the higher points in May; summer catches from the deeper edges. Late May-June: fish rocky structure in 15-20ft. Summer: push to deeper edges 20-30ft. Access from Sturgeon Point Marina (618 Sturgeon Point Rd, Derby NY 14047). Coordinates from fishingstatus.com map embed (42.65833N, 79.08333W) — approximate reef center. Verify shoal top with depth finder on approach.
source: fishingstatus.com spot listing (IndexID 1420562), NYSDEC Lake Erie Smallmouth Bass Hotspots, anyplaceamerica.com GNIS (Sturgeon Point: 42.6908919N, 79.0475387W)

name: Sturgeon Point Nearshore (Derby / Angola Shoal)
notes: Sturgeon Point cape creates a nearshore rock and rubble zone that concentrates smallmouth spring through fall. The rocky point extends into the lake creating a natural current seam and depth break. Erie County Site #41 confirms: rock bass, smallmouth bass, bluegill, yellow perch, rainbow trout. Sturgeon Point Marina provides direct boat launch access (618 Sturgeon Point Rd, Derby NY 14047). The nearshore shoal extends SW toward Evans Bar offshore. Spring spawning fish use the 5-12ft rocky areas around the point. The NYSDEC Lake Erie eastern basin fishing map covers this corridor from the NY/PA line to Sturgeon Point (Fishing Hot Spots map M484).
source: anyplaceamerica.com GNIS (42.6908919N, 79.0475387W), Erie County Western NY Fishing hotspot #41, NYSDEC Lake Erie Smallmouth Bass Hotspots
